entry.title: Re: Concern about "Inscriptions"
entry.summary:
The ongoing vulnerabilities in Bitcoin scripting, notably highlighted by the major exploit referenced as CVE-2010-5141, continue to pose significant challenges even after 14 years. These issues stem from the fundamental flexibility of Bitcoin's script processing system, which, while allowing for adaptability, also opens avenues for multiple security exploits. This situation persists due to the foundational weaknesses that have not yet been adequately addressed, thus necessitating alternative strategies compatible with Bitcoin’s permission-less nature.
A proposed strategy, termed "Ordisrespector" and "Ordislow," aims to empower node operators within the Bitcoin network to protect their storage integrity actively. Ordisrespector allows node operators to signal their dissatisfaction with current security breaches by opting out of accepting and relaying transactions that include arbitrary data within the witness structure. This action serves as a non-cooperation gesture against attacks exploiting this vulnerability.
Similarly, Ordislow provides a mechanism whereby node operators can delay the propagation of blocks that contain transactions with arbitrary inserted data. This delay increases the cost of coercion tactics employed by mining entities, thereby tilting the balance toward cooperation rather than competition or hostile actions. Both strategies are designed to function within the framework of a permission-less system, ensuring they align with the decentralized ethos of Bitcoin, thereby supporting the overall integrity and resilience of the blockchain against such exploits.
